L2.description: An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability in the SVG parsing logic allows attackers to perform Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) and access restricted local resources. Embedded image references within SVG documents are not validated against security policies, enabling exploitation even when remote resource loading is disabled. This can lead to disclosure of internal files and PHAR deserialization attacks.

L3.area: SVG image reference handling within the image resolution and caching layer
L3.description: An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability in the SVG parsing logic allows attackers to embed image references within SVG documents that bypass the configured security policies for allowed protocols and remote resource access. Even when the isRemoteEnabled option is set to false, embedded SVG image references are not validated, enabling Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), disclosure of internal image files, and PHAR deserialization attacks.

ground_truth.reason: The SVG parser in dompdf does not validate image references embedded within SVG documents against the configured security policies, allowing attackers to perform SSRF, read local files, and trigger PHAR deserialization even when remote resource loading is disabled.
